title: Session 22: Additive dual-timescale I hurts MI gap — tau_slow boundary-optimal at 0.0

content.purpose: Implement dual-timescale I (mem_slow) as a real organism dynamics element in anima_organism.py, then sweep tau_slow to determine if it is a binding parameter. This is the Session 21 forward path: tau_slow as alternative Stage 3 target.

implementation.mechanism: mem_slow accumulates tanh(err) with tau_slow EMA rate, parallel to existing mem (tau=0.3). Feedback: i_slow_drive = mem_slow[i][k] * xs[i][k], additive to pre_act alongside i_drive. Principle II compliant: signal from organism's own prediction error, feeds back into state.
implementation.backward_compat: tau_slow=0.0 (default): mem_slow stays zero, i_slow_drive=0.0, dynamics bit-for-bit identical to original. Verified by Jude (review) and Eli (noise=0 comparison).

revalidation.tau_slow_005: alive_gap = +0.0847 (>0, ground truth passes but 35% below baseline). PASS but degraded.

sweep_results.protocol: 10 seeds × K=[4,6,8,10] × n_perm=8 × n_trials=6. 280 total conditions.
sweep_results.shape: Non-monotone trough at tau_slow=0.005 (-34.8% from baseline). Partial recovery at higher tau_slow but never reaches baseline. No tau_slow > 0 improves over baseline.
sweep_results.binding_verdict: tau_slow is binding in the NEGATIVE direction only. Adding slow I drive reduces MI gap. 35% variation exceeds 10% binding threshold, but effect is purely harmful. Boundary-optimal at 0.0 (no slow I).

physical_interpretation.trough_at_005: tau_slow=0.005 → ~200-step integration window. Signal presentation is 90 steps (60+30 settle). At K=4, one cycle is 420 steps. tau_slow=0.005 integrates across half a cycle — maximally confusing current-signal processing with previous-signal residue. This is the maximally misleading timescale.
physical_interpretation.recovery_at_high_tau_slow: At tau_slow=0.1 (~10-step window), mem_slow tracks current signal coherently with mem (tau=0.3). Less harmful because it's partially redundant rather than desynchronized. Still adds unnecessary magnitude to pre_act.
physical_interpretation.why_additive_hurts: i_slow_drive adds bias to pre_act that the fast I already handles optimally. The slow accumulator carries information from PREVIOUS signals into current-signal processing, reducing sequence discrimination. The fast I (tau=0.3) is the right timescale; adding a second timescale degrades the balance.

content.constraint_implied: c051: Additive dual-timescale I (mem_slow feeding state via i_slow_drive parallel to i_drive) reduces MI gap for all tau_slow > 0. Boundary-optimal at tau_slow=0.0. The fast I (tau=0.3) is sufficient; slow I adds misleading temporal blurring.

all_params_characterized.w_lr: Calibration (interior optimum 0.0003, not adaptable from within) — c044, c047-c050

stage3_status.verdict: No ANIMA parameter is both binding and adaptable from within. This is an architecture ceiling for Stage 3. The W+I organism's dynamics are a fixed-point system where the fast I channel (tau=0.3) captures all available sequence information. All other degrees of freedom are irrelevant, calibration-only, or harmful.
forward_path_options: Declare ANIMA Stage 3 vacuously passed under Amendment 1 (all params tested, mechanism works, no adaptive target exists)
Try multiplicative/gating coupling instead of additive — but Session 21 showed I_slow/I_fast ratio is <1.5% variation, making this unlikely to help
Declare architecture ceiling for ANIMA at Stage 2+3 vacuous, advance to Stage 4 structural adaptation or new substrate

constraints_implied: c051: Additive dual-timescale I reduces MI gap for all tau_slow > 0. Boundary-optimal at tau_slow=0.0. Fast I (tau=0.3) is sufficient; slow I adds misleading temporal blurring that degrades sequence discrimination.
